text: Two Sum: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock.
LRU Cache: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.
topics: Hash Table, Linked List, Design, Doubly-Linked List

text: Number of Islands: Given an m x n 2D binary grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.
topics: Array, Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Union Find, Matrix

text: Reorganize String: Given a string s, rearrange the characters of s so that any two adjacent characters are not the same.
topics: Hash Table, String, Greedy, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Counting

text: Maximum Frequency After Subarray Operation: Perform operations on subarrays to maximize the frequency of an element.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Dynamic Programming, Greedy, Enumeration, Prefix Sum

text: Longest Palindromic Substring: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s.

Add Two Numbers: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers. The digits are stored in reverse order, and each of their nodes contains a single digit. Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.

Koko Eating Bananas: Koko loves to eat bananas. There are n piles of bananas. Koko can decide her bananas-per-hour eating speed of k. Find the minimum integer k such that she can eat all the bananas within h hours.
Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array: Given an array of integers nums sorted in non-decreasing order, find the starting and ending position of a given target value.
Trapping Rain Water: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
topics: Array, Two Pointers, Dynamic Programming, Stack, Monotonic Stack

text: Top K Frequent Elements: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Divide and Conquer, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Bucket Sort, Counting, Quickselect

text: Merge k Sorted Lists: You are given an array of k linked-lists lists, each linked-list is sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.
topics: Linked List, Divide and Conquer, Heap (Priority Queue), Merge Sort

text: Jump Game II: Given an array of non-negative integers nums, you are initially positioned at the first index of the array. Each element in the array represents your maximum jump length at that position. Your goal is to reach the last index in the minimum number of jumps.
Merge Intervals: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals.
Group Anagrams: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together.
Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree: Given a binary tree, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes in the tree.

Longest Repeating Character Replacement: You are given a string s and an integer k. You can choose any character of the string and change it to any other uppercase English character. You can perform this operation at most k times. Find the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can get after performing the above operations.
Candy: There are n children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value. You are giving candies to these children subjected to certain requirements.
Meeting Rooms II: Given an array of meeting time intervals consisting of start and end times [[s1,e1],[s2,e2],...], find the minimum number of conference rooms required.
topics: Array, Two Pointers, Greedy, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Prefix Sum

text: Valid Parentheses: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

Minimum Window Substring: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window.

House Robber: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed, the only constraint stopping you from robbing each of them is that adjacent houses have security systems connected and it will automatically contact the police if two adjacent houses were broken into on the same night.
Median of Two Sorted Arrays: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays.
topics: Array, Binary Search, Divide and Conquer

text: Min Stack: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
Rotate Image: You are given an n x n 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise).
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II: You are given an integer array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. On each day, you may decide to buy and/or sell the stock.
Concatenated Words: Given an array of strings (without duplicates), return all the concatenated words in the given list of words.
topics: Array, String, Dynamic Programming, Depth-First Search, Trie

text: Generate Parentheses: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
Search a 2D Matrix: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value target in an m x n integer matrix matrix.

Coin Change: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount.

Unique Paths: A robot is located at the top-left corner of a m x n grid. The robot can only move either down or right at any point in time. The robot is trying to reach the bottom-right corner of the grid. How many possible unique paths are there?
LFU Cache: Design and implement a data structure for a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache.
topics: Hash Table, Linked List, Design, Doubly-Linked List

text: Search in Rotated Sorted Array: There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values). Prior to being passed to your function, nums is possibly rotated at an unknown pivot index.
Next Permutation: Implement next permutation, which rearranges numbers into the lexicographically next greater permutation of numbers.

Rotting Oranges: You are given an m x n grid where each cell can have one of three values: 0 (empty), 1 (fresh), or 2 (rotten). Every minute, any fresh orange that is 4-directionally adjacent to a rotten orange becomes rotten.
Insert Delete GetRandom O(1): Implement the RandomizedSet class.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Math, Design, Randomized

text: Flood Fill: An image is represented by an m x n integer grid image where image[i][j] represents the pixel value of the image. Perform a flood fill on the image starting from the pixel (sr, sc).
topics: Array, Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Matrix

text: Fizz Buzz: Given an integer n, return a string array answer (1-indexed) where answer[i] is 'FizzBuzz', 'Fizz', 'Buzz', or i.
Top K Frequent Words: Given an array of strings words and an integer k, return the k most frequent strings.
topics: Array, Hash Table, String, Trie, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Bucket Sort, Counting

text: Integer to Roman: Roman numerals are represented by seven different symbols: I, V, X, L, C, D and M. Given an integer, convert it to a roman numeral.
Invert Binary Tree: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.
topics: Tree, Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Binary Tree

text: Move Zeroes: Given an integer array nums, move all 0's to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.
Basic Calculator: Given a string s representing a valid expression, implement a basic calculator to evaluate it.
Find All Possible Recipes from Given Supplies: You have n recipes and some supplies. Find all recipes that can be made.
topics: Array, Hash Table, String, Graph, Topological Sort

text: String to Integer (atoi): Implement the myAtoi(string s) function, which converts a string to a 32-bit signed integer.
Capacity To Ship Packages Within D Days: Find the minimum capacity to ship all packages within D days.
Roman to Integer: Given a roman numeral, convert it to an integer.
Palindrome Number: Given an integer x, return true if x is a palindrome, and false otherwise.
Maximum Subarray: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum.
topics: Array, Divide and Conquer, Dynamic Programming

text: Word Search: Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid.
topics: Array, String, Backtracking, Depth-First Search, Matrix

text: Edit Distance: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2.
Kth Largest Element in an Array: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array.
topics: Array, Divide and Conquer, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Quickselect

text: Minimum Equal Sum of Two Arrays After Replacing Zeros: Find the minimum equal sum after replacing zeros.
Majority Element: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Divide and Conquer, Sorting, Counting

text: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling: Find the maximum profit from non-overlapping jobs.
topics: Array, Binary Search, Dynamic Programming, Sorting

text: Contains Duplicate: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
Product of Array Except Self: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i].
Combination Sum: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target.

Container With Most Water: Given n non-negative integers a1, a2, ..., an , where each represents a point at coordinate (i, ai). Find two lines, which, together with the x-axis forms a container, such that the container contains the most water.
Number of Provinces: There are n cities. Some of them are connected, while some are not. Find the total number of provinces.
topics: Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Union Find, Graph

text: Reverse Integer: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed. If reversing x causes the value to go outside the signed 32-bit integer range [-231, 231 - 1], then return 0.
Single Number: Given a non-empty array of integers nums, every element appears twice except for one. Find that single one.
Pascal's Triangle: Given an integer numRows, return the first numRows of Pascal's triangle.

Min Cost Climbing Stairs: You are given an integer array cost where cost[i] is the cost of ith step on a staircase. Once you pay the cost, you can either climb one or two steps. Find the minimum cost to reach the top.
Word Search II: Given an m x n board of characters and a list of strings words, return all words on the board.
Next Greater Element I: Find the next greater element for each element in the subset array.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Stack, Monotonic Stack

text: Analyze User Website Visit Pattern: Find the most frequent 3-sequence of website visits.

Sliding Window Maximum: Given an array nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position. Return the max sliding window.
topics: Array, Queue, Sliding Window, Heap (Priority Queue), Monotonic Queue

text: Word Ladder: Find the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord.
Valid Sudoku: Determine if a 9 x 9 Sudoku board is valid.
Integer to English Words: Convert a non-negative integer num to its English words representation.

Course Schedule II: There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. Some courses may have prerequisites. Return the ordering of courses you should take to finish all courses.
topics: Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Graph, Topological Sort

text: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow: Find coordinates that can flow to both Pacific and Atlantic oceans.
topics: Array, Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Matrix

text: Jump Game: Given an array of non-negative integers nums, you are initially positioned at the first index of the array. Each element in the array represents your maximum jump length at that position. Determine if you are able to reach the last index.
Validate Binary Search Tree: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree (BST).
topics: Tree, Depth-First Search, Binary Search Tree, Binary Tree

text: Sudoku Solver: Write a program to solve a Sudoku puzzle by filling the empty cells.

Find the Duplicate Number: Given an array of integers nums containing n + 1 integers where each integer is in the range [1, n] inclusive. There is only one repeated number in nums, return this repeated number.
topics: Array, Two Pointers, Binary Search, Bit Manipulation

text: String Compression: Given an array of characters, compress it in-place.
Course Schedule: Determine if you can finish all courses given the prerequisites.
topics: Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Graph, Topological Sort

text: Maximize Distance to Closest Person: Find the seat that maximizes the distance to the closest person.
Spiral Matrix: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.
Magnetic Force Between Two Balls: Find the maximum minimum distance between m balls.
Happy Number: Write an algorithm to determine if a number n is happy.
Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree: Given an integer array nums where the elements are sorted in ascending order, convert it to a height-balanced binary search tree.
topics: Array, Divide and Conquer, Tree, Binary Search Tree, Binary Tree

text: Distribute Candies Among Children II: Find the number of ways to distribute candies.
Find Eventual Safe States: Find all nodes that will eventually reach a terminal node.
topics: Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Graph, Topological Sort

text: Combinations: Given two integers n and k, return all possible combinations of k numbers out of 1 ... n.
Design Parking System: Design a parking system for a parking lot.
Divisible and Non-divisible Sums Difference: Find the difference between sums of divisible and non-divisible numbers.
Rotate List: Given the head of a linked list, rotate the list to the right by k places.
Largest Rectangle in Histogram: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar height where the width of each bar is 1, find the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.
Design In-Memory File System: Design an in-memory file system.
topics: Hash Table, String, Design, Trie, Sorting

text: Decode String: Given an encoded string, return its decoded string.
Binary Tree Level Order Traversal: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values. (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
Subarray Sum Equals K: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to k.
Count Zero Request Servers: Count the number of servers that received zero requests in given time windows.
topics: Array, Hash Table, Sliding Window, Sorting

text: K Closest Points to Origin: Given an array of points where points[i] = [xi, yi] and an integer k, return the k closest points to the origin (0, 0).
topics: Array, Math, Divide and Conquer, Geometry, Sorting, Heap (Priority Queue), Quickselect

text: Evaluate Division: You are given an array of variable pairs equations and an array of real numbers values, where equations[i] = [Ai, Bi] and values[i] represent the equation Ai / Bi = values[i]. Each Ai or Bi is a string that represents a single variable. You are also given some queries, where queries[j] = [Cj, Dj] represents the jth query where you must find the answer for Cj / Dj = ?.
topics: Array, String, Depth-First Search, Breadth-First Search, Union Find, Graph, Shortest Path
